REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 200013754) FILED BY DEFENDANT BIBO, MARK DENES
LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MARK BIBO NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS OFF CALENDAR DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARK BIBO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON APRIL 2, 2020 IS GRANTED."ASIDE FROM STATUTE, COURTS HAVE AN INHERENT POWER TO CORRECT JUDGMENTS WHERE THERE HAS BEEN A CLERICAL ERROR BY THE CLERK . . . THERE IS NO TIME LIMIT WITHIN WHICH THIS CORRECTION NEED BE MADE . . . [T]HE POWER TO AMEND . . . EXTENDS . . . TO CASES WHERE SOME PROVISION OF . . . AN ORDER OR JUDGMENT AS MADE OR RENDERED WAS DUE TO THE INADVERTENCE OR MISTAKE OF THE COURT AND MAY THEREFORE PROPERLY BE TREATED AS A CLERICAL MISPRISION." (MORGAN V. STATE BD. OF EQUALIZATION (1949) 89 CAL.APP.2D 674, 677.) PURSUANT TO THE COVID-19 EMERGENCY RULES AND COURT HOLIDAYS, DEFENDANT'S RESPONSIVE PLEADING WAS NOT YET DUE ON APRIL 2, AND IT WAS AN ERROR FOR DEFAULT TO BE ENTERED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Set For Trial On Jan-10-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Nikolaus Reed, Esq. of the Law Office of Nikolaus S. Reed, 415.940.7766, appearing for the Plaintiff Deneen Myisha Jones;
Atty. Alexander MacDougall, Esq. of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh, 925.825.9500, appearing for Defendant Mark Denes Bibo.

DEFENDANT MARK BIBO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Mark Bibo's frivolous motion to strike punitive damages from the complaint is denied. The allegations in the complaint readily support Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages. Civil Code section 3294(a) limits punitive damages to circumstances "where it is proven by clear and convincing evidence that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice." "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code Sec. 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.) Here, the complaint alleges the defendant "intentionally rammed plaintiff with his vehicle multiple times, after threatening to 'rip [her] fucking head off.'" (Compl., p. 7, EX-2.) The facts allege defendant engaged in an intentional tort-indeed, in criminal assault with a deadly weapon (a motor vehicle); therefore, the motion to strike is denied. (See Lackner v. North (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1188, 1212 [punitive damages "are typically awarded for intentional torts such as assault and battery . . . ."]; Ingram v. Higgins (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 287, 290 [affirming award of punitive damages for assault by motorist who drove car against police officer, causing personal injuries]; see also People v. Russell (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 776, 782 ["The law makes clear a person who operates or drives a vehicle in an attempt to injure another person has committed [criminal] assault with a deadly weapon, to wit, the car."]; Veh. Code Sec. 13210 [suspension of driving privileges for assault commonly known as "road rage"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Nikolaus Reed, Esq. of the Law Office of Nikolaus S. Reed, 415.940.7766, appearing for the Plaintiff Deneen Myisha Jones;
Atty. Alexander MacDougall, Esq. of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh, 925.825.9500, appearing for Defendant Mark Denes Bibo.

DEFENDANT MARK BIBO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter called, NO ARGUMENT is held. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Mark Bibo's frivolous demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is overruled. Plaintiff properly alleges the ultimate facts of the intentional tort, assault and battery. (See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872 ["To survive a demurrer, the complaint need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged."].) As long as the essential facts of some valid cause of action are alleged, the complaint is good against a general demurrer. (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39.) Plaintiff alleges that while driving, she merged lanes in front of defendant. (Compl., p. 6, para. 2.) Defendant made threatening comments that he was going to "rip [her] fucking head off." (Id.) Defendant then collided into the rear of plaintiff's car, reversed, and rammed into her again. (Id. at par. 3.) "Defendant intentionally used his vehicle as a deadly weapon", threatened plaintiff, intended to cause her harm, and did cause her actual harm. (Id. par. 4.) Thus, the ultimate facts are alleged for a claim of assault and battery. (See So v. Shin (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 652, 668-669 [elements of assault and battery].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Entry Of Default And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Off Calendar

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Entry Of Default And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Alexander Macdougall
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARK BIBO's Motion To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Entry Of Default And Request For Sanctions.

Defendant's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 2, 2020 is granted.

"Aside from statute, courts have an inherent power to correct judgments where there has been a clerical error by the clerk . . . There is no time limit within which this correction need be made . . . [T]he power to amend . . . extends . . . to cases where some provision of . . . an order or judgment as made or rendered was due to the inadvertence or mistake of the court and may therefore properly be treated as a clerical misprision." (Morgan v. State Bd. of Equalization (1949) 89 Cal.App.2d 674, 677.) Pursuant to the COVID-19 emergency rules and court holidays, Defendant's responsive pleading was not yet due on April 2, and it was an error for default to be entered. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
